data.name: Age and Sex Requirement
data.text: Female (regardless of menopausal status) or male ≥18 years of age (or of an acceptable age according to local regulations, whichever is older).

data.name: Confirmed HR+, HER2_negative, early stage resected invasive breast cancer
data.text:
The patient has confirmed HR+, HER2_negative (HER2_), early stage resected invasive breast cancer without evidence of distant metastases.
 To fulfill the requirement for HR+ disease by local testing on primary disease specimen, tumor must be ER or PgR positive defined by immunohistochemistry (IHC) according to American Society of Clinical Oncology (ASCO)/College of American Pathologists (CAP) guidelines for hormone receptor testing (Hammond et al. 2010).
 To fulfill the requirement of HER2_ disease by local testing on primary disease specimen, tumor must be HER2_ according to ASCO/CAP guidelines for HER2 testing (Wolff et al. 2018).
Patients with bilateral breast cancer (diagnosis of invasive tumors in both breasts simultaneously or within 6 months of each other) can be eligible if all lesions tested on both sides are HR+/HER2_ and adequate surgery has been performed in both breasts (see inclusion criterion [3]). The Lilly Clinical Research Physician and Clinical Research Scientist (CRP/CRS) must be consulted for all cases of bilateral breast cancer.

data.name: Definitive surgery of primary breast tumor(
data.text:
The patient must have undergone definitive surgery of the primary breast tumor(s).
 With the exception of the situations described below, the margins of the resected specimen must be histologically free of invasive tumor and /or a component of ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS) as determined by the local pathologist. If pathologic examination demonstrates tumor at the line of resection, additional excisions may be performed to obtain clear margins. If tumor is still present at the resected margin after re_excision(s), the patient must undergo mastectomy to be eligible. Of note, patients with margins positive for lobular carcinoma in situ (LCIS) are eligible without additional resection.
 For patients who undergo mastectomy or wide local excision where deep margin abuts the pectoralis fascia, patients with microscopic positive margins are eligible as long as radiotherapy of the chest wall is administered prior to study entry. Patients with positive anterior margins may be eligible if there is no gross disease left behind (radiotherapy as per local guidelines).
 Where surgical excision of supraclavicular or internal mammary nodes is not feasible, residual nodes should be irradiated in accordance with standard guidelines.
 If given, radiation therapy (for example, post_mastectomy or post_lumpectomy) should be administered according to standard guidelines.

data.name: Tumor tissue for exploratory biomarker analysis
data.text:
The patient must have tumor tissue from breast (preferred) or lymph node for exploratory biomarker analysis available prior to randomization. 
Note: Sites should confirm the availability of tumor tissue for exploratory analysis (Section 9.8.2.2) with the pathological laboratory prior to randomization

data.name: Node positive status and additional criteria
data.text:
Patients must be node positive (microscopic and macroscopic tumor involvement are allowed; ipsilateral internal mammary and supraclavicular lymph nodes are allowed, but will not count toward the number of positive lymph nodes) and fulfill one of the following criteria: 
A. Pathological tumor involvement in ≥4 ipsilateral axillary lymph nodes.
OR
B. Pathological tumor involvement in 1 to 3 ipsilateral axillary lymph node(s) (for patients who received neoadjuvant therapy also cytological tumor involvement at time of initial diagnosis is allowed) and meet at least 1 of the following criteria:
1. Grade 3 as defined by a combined score of at least 8 points per the modified Bloom_Richardson grading system (Elston and Ellis 1991), also known as the Nottingham scale, or equivalent following discussion with the Lilly CRP/CRS
– pathological primary invasive tumor size ≥5 cm (for patients who received neoadjuvant therapy primary tumor size ≥5 cm on breast imaging is allowed). Note: if tumor size is needed to meet eligibility criteria, patients with multifocal/multicentric tumors may be eligible based on the addition of diameters of the individual lesions following discussion with the Lilly CRP/CRS.
– Ki_67 index of ≥20% (for Cohort 2) on untreated breast tissue as determined by the investigational assay (described in Section 3.2.1) at the Study JPCF central laboratory. See Section 9.8.1 for Ki_67 sample requirements.

data.name: Primary Efficacy Objective
data.text: To evaluate the efficacy, in terms of IDFS, for patients with HR+, HER2_ early stage breast cancer for abemaciclib plus adjuvant endocrine therapy versus adjuvant endocrine therapy alone.

data.name: Secondary Efficacy Objective (Ki_67 subgrou
data.text: To evaluate the efficacy, in terms of IDFS, for patients with HR+, HER2_ early stage breast cancer with pretreatment Ki_67 index ≥20% by central lab

data.name: Secondary Efficacy Objective (DRFS and OS)
data.text: To evaluate the efficacy of abemaciclib plus adjuvant endocrine therapy versus adjuvant endocrine therapy alone in terms of DRFS and OS

data.name: Secondary Safety Objective
data.text: To assess the safety profile of abemaciclib plus adjuvant endocrine therapy compared to adjuvant endocrine therapy alone

data.name: Secondary Pharmacokinetic/Clinical Outcomes Relationship Objective
data.text: To evaluate the relationship between abemaciclib, exposure and clinical (efficacy and safety) outcomes

data.name: Secondary Quality of Life Objective
data.text: To evaluate abemaciclib plus adjuvant endocrine therapy, versus adjuvant endocrine therapy alone, in terms of general oncology and breast cancer self_reported health_related quality of life (FACT_B 37_item questionnaire), endocrine therapy_specific symptoms (the FACT_ES 19_item subscale and 2 FACIT_sourced items of cognitive symptoms and 3 FACIT_sourced items for bladder symptoms), and fatigue experienced during abemaciclib and/or endocrine therapy (the FACIT_F 13_item subscale).

data.name: Secondary Health Status/Economic Evaluation Objective
data.text: To evaluate health status to inform decision modeling for health economic evaluation using the EQ_5D_5L.

raw.name: Informed consent (Cohort 2 and/or main consent)
raw.description: Main ICF may be signed up to 3 months prior to randomization and must be signed prior to any study_specific assessments are performed. Cohort 2 ICF may be signed up to 6 months prior to randomization and must be signed prior to sending tissue to the central lab for Ki_67 testing.

raw.name: Blood pregnancy test
raw.description: See inclusion criteria [12], Appendix 4. Women of reproductive potential only. Local regulations and/or institutional guidelines may require additional testing.

raw.name: Physical exam (clinic visits only)
raw.description: Height, weight, and appropriate body examination. Height at baseline only and weight until STFU. Does not need to be repeated on Day 1 if assessed ≤7 days prior to randomization.

raw.name: Vital signs (clinic visits only)
raw.description: Blood pressure, pulse, respiratory rate, temperature. Do not need to be repeated on Day 1 if assessed ≤7 days prior to randomization.

raw.name: ECOG PS (clinic visits only)
raw.description: See Appendix 11. Does not need to be repeated on Day 1 if assessed ≤7 days prior to randomization.

raw.name: Medical history
raw.description: Includes alcohol and tobacco intake.

raw.name: Concomitant medications
raw.description: In LTFU, collection of regimen changes and associated start and stop dates for endocrine therapy, and post_discontinuation therapy.

raw.name: ECG (local)
raw.description: As clinically indicated Patient must be supine or near supine for approximately 5_10 minutes prior to collection and remain supine, but awake, during collection.

raw.name: Adverse event assessment
raw.description: See Section 9.2. Assessments will be in clinic and over the phone between clinic visits. In LTFU, all SAEs in both arms regardless of causality will be collected through Year 5. For the entire duration of LTFU, only AEs related to study treatment and/or study procedure will be collected.

data.name: Central hematology, chemistry, and cystatin C (clinic visits onl

raw.name: Central hematology, chemistry, and cystatin C (clinic visits only)
raw.description: See Appendix 3. Do not need to be repeated on Day 1 if assessed ≤7 days prior to randomization (blood draw is required at Day 1 for pharmacogenetic and biomarker sample collection – see Appendix 6). Eligibility must be based on the last available screening laboratory results prior to randomization. Enrollment and treatment decisions may be based on local laboratory results, but a specimen must also be sent to central laboratory. Discrepancies between local and central laboratory results will not be considered protocol deviations.

raw.name: PRO questionnaires
raw.description: See Section 9.9.

data.name: Bilateral breast imaging (such as mammogram or MRI)

raw.name: Bilateral breast imaging (such as mammogram or MRI)
raw.description: Prior to randomization: Patients must have bilateral breast imaging performed locally as part of routine standard of care prior to surgery or prior to randomization for the current breast cancer. This is not required for patients who have had bilateral mastectomy. Post randomization: Either at yearly intervals, as recommended per international standard guidelines, or according to local standards as part of routine medical care. Patients who have had a mastectomy should be followed per local practice. See Appendix 10 for additional guidelines for the assessment of recurrence.

data.name: Abdominal ± pelvic imaging (such as CT, PET/CT, MRI, ultrasoun

raw.name: Abdominal ± pelvic imaging (such as CT, PET/CT, MRI, ultrasound)
raw.description: Prior to randomization: Abdominal ± pelvic imaging MUST be performed prior to randomization. Abdominal ± pelvic imaging performed previously as part of routine care, any time in the process of or after diagnosing the patient with the current breast cancer diagnosis, may be used as the baseline assessment. Post randomization: Performed locally ONLY if clinically indicated per investigator’s judgment (eg, if liver function tests deteriorate significantly). If PET/CT is performed, additional imaging modalities are not required. See Appendix 10 for additional guidelines for the assessment of recurrence.

data.name: Chest imaging (such as PET/CT, CT, or x_ray [should include lateral and postero_anterior views])

raw.name: Chest imaging (such as PET/CT, CT, or x_ray [should include lateral and postero_anterior views])
raw.description: Prior to randomization: Chest imaging MUST be performed prior to randomization. Chest imaging performed previously as part of routine care, any time in the process of or after diagnosing the patient with the current breast cancer diagnosis, may be used as the baseline assessment. Post randomization: Performed locally ONLY if clinically indicated per the investigator’s judgment. If PET/CT is performed, additional imaging modalities are not required. See Appendix 10 for additional guidelines for the assessment of recurrence.

data.name: Bone nuclear imaging (such as bone scan, PET scan or PET/CT)

raw.name: Bone nuclear imaging (such as bone scan, PET scan or PET/CT)
raw.description: Prior to randomization: Full body bone imaging per standard clinical practice MUST be performed prior to randomization to discard or confirm potential distant disease. Bone imaging performed previously as part of routine care, any time in the process of or after diagnosing the patient with the current breast cancer diagnosis, may be used as the baseline assessment. Post randomization: Performed locally and ONLY if clinically indicated per investigator’s judgment (for example, if patient is symptomatic for bone pain and/or if alkaline phosphatase is significantly elevated ≥3×ULN). If PET/CT is performed, additional imaging modalities are not required. See Appendix 10 for additional guidelines for the assessment of recurrence.

raw.name: Administer abemaciclib (Arm A)
raw.description: See Sections 7.1 and 7.7.1.1. Orally twice_daily (with minimum 6_hour separating doses) for up to 2 years or until discontinuation criteria met.

raw.name: Administer endocrine therapy
raw.description: See inclusion criterion [7] In STFU and LTFU, as applicable. Administered as per physician’s choice.

raw.name: Disease recurrence assessment
raw.description: See Section 9.1 and Appendix 10. Disease recurrence information will be collected at any time point (clinic or phone visit). Assessment for changes in symptoms and suggestive of disease recurrence.

raw.name: Ki_67 tissue (untreated breast tissue)
raw.description: See Section 9.8.1 and Appendix 6. As assessed by central laboratory when kit is available for both cohorts. Screening for potential Cohort 2 patients is permitted after signing the Cohort 2 ICF ((tissue should be submitted within 1 month from consent date).

data.name: Ki_67 and exploratory biomarker tissue (post_neoadjuvant therap

raw.name: Ki_67 and exploratory biomarker tissue (post_neoadjuvant therapy)
raw.description: See Section 9.8.1 and Appendix 6. In addition to pretreatment sample, if available, send to central laboratory only for patients who received neoadjuvant therapy. Not applicable for patients who had a pCR following neoadjuvant therapy.

raw.name: Tumor tissue for biomarkers
raw.description: See Section 9.8 and Appendix 6. Confirm sample availability prior to randomization. Breast tissue preferred; lymph node tissue is acceptable. For patients who received neoadjuvant therapy, send both a pre_ and a post–neoadjuvant treatment tissue sample to the central laboratory (unless the patient had a pCR).

raw.name: Plasma for biomarker
raw.description: See Section 9.8 and Appendix 6. At Day 1, must be taken before first dose. V802 sample may be taken after V802 if missed at V802. Y5 sample for patients on study without IDFS event

raw.name: Pharmacokinetics
raw.description: Only for patients in Arm A assigned to PK in IWRS. See Section 9.5 and Appendix 6. Ask the patient and record date and time of last abemaciclib dose prior to PK sample. V1 sample, 2 hours after abemaciclib dosing. Other visits, any time after abemaciclib dosing.

data.description: Elevation in AST/ALT >3x ULN with total bilirubin >2x ULN in absence of cholestasis requires discontinuation
data.text: Elevation in AST and/or ALT >3 x ULN with total bilirubin >2 x ULN, in the absence of cholestasis: Abemaciclib therapy MUST be discontinued.

data.name: ILD/Pneumonitis Grade 3_4 Discontinuation
data.description: Grade 3 or 4 ILD/pneumonitis requires abemaciclib discontinuation
data.text: ILD/pneumonitis Grade 3 or Grade 4: Abemaciclib therapy MUST be discontinued.

data.name: Significant Noncompliance
data.description: Patient repeatedly misses >25% of abemaciclib doses or takes ≥125% of prescribed amount
data.text: A patient will be considered significantly noncompliant if she or he repeatedly misses more than 25% of abemaciclib doses between scheduled clinic visits. Similarly, a patient will be considered significantly noncompliant if she or he is judged by the investigator to have intentionally or repeatedly taken more than (≥125%) the prescribed amount of medication.

data.name: IDFS Event per STEEP Criteria
data.description: Occurrence of invasive disease_free survival event per STEEP criteria
data.text: The patient experiences any of the IDFS events as per STEEP criteria.

data.name: Lowest Dose Requiring Further Reduction
data.description: Patient on 50 mg twice daily requiring further dose reduction must discontinue abemaciclib
data.text: If a patient receiving the 50_mg twice daily dose of abemaciclib requires further dose reduction, the patient must be discontinued from abemaciclib.

data.name: Hematologic Toxicity Grade 3 Dose Suspension
data.description: Grade 3 hematologic toxicity requires dose suspension until resolution to at least Grade 2
data.text: Hematologic Toxicity Grade 3: Dose MUST be suspended until toxicity resolves to at least Grade 2.

data.name: Recurrent Grade 3 or Grade 4 Hematologic Toxicity
data.description: Recurrent Grade 3 or Grade 4 hematologic toxicity requires dose suspension and reduction
data.text: Hematologic Toxicity Recurrent Grade 3 or Grade 4: Dose MUST be suspended until toxicity resolves to at least Grade 2. Dose MUST be reduced by 1 dose level.

data.name: Diarrhea Grade 2 Not Resolving Within 24 Hours
data.description: Grade 2 diarrhea not resolving within 24 hours requires dose suspension
data.text: Diarrhea Grade 2 that does not resolve within 24 hours to at least Grade 1: Dose MUST be suspended until toxicity resolves to at least Grade 1.

data.name: Non_hematologic Toxicity Persistent/Recurrent Grade 2 or Grade 3_4
data.description: Persistent or recurrent Grade 2 not resolving within 7 days, or Grade 3_4 requires suspension and reduction
data.text: Non_hematologic Toxicity: Persistent or recurrent Grade 2 that does not resolve with maximal supportive measures within 7 days to baseline or Grade 1, or Grade 3 or 4: Dose MUST be suspended until toxicity resolves to either baseline or Grade 1. Dose MUST be reduced by 1 dose level.

data.name: Dose Suspension >28 Days Requires Discussion
data.description: Dose suspensions of 28 days or more must be discussed with Lilly CRP/CRS
data.text: Dose suspensions ≥28 days must be discussed with Lilly CRP/CRS.

data.name: Screening to On_Study Treatment Transition
data.text: Patient must meet all inclusion criteria and no exclusion criteria. First dose of study treatment must be taken no later than 3 days after randomization. Eligibility must be based on the last available screening laboratory results prior to randomization.

data.name: On_Study Treatment to Short_Term Follow_Up Transition
data.text: Short_term follow_up visit takes place 30 days (±5 days) after completion of the 2_year on_study treatment period or after discontinuation criteria are met and decision is made for the patient to discontinue all study treatment, whichever occurs first.

data.name: Short_Term Follow_Up to Long_Term Follow_Up Transition
data.text: The long_term follow_up period begins the day after the short_term follow_up visit. ALL patients will enter the long_term follow_up period.

data.name: Discontinuation for ALT/AST Grade 4
data.text: ALT/AST Increased Grade 4 (>20.0×ULN): Abemaciclib therapy MUST be discontinued.

data.name: Discontinuation for Hy's Law Criteria
data.text: Elevation in AST and/or ALT >3 x ULN with total bilirubin >2 x ULN, in the absence of cholestasis: Abemaciclib therapy MUST be discontinued.

data.name: Discontinuation for ILD/Pneumonitis Grade 3_4
data.text: ILD/pneumonitis Grade 3 or Grade 4: Abemaciclib therapy MUST be discontinued.

data.name: Discontinuation for Lowest Dose Requiring Further Reduction
data.text: If a patient receiving the 50_mg twice daily dose of abemaciclib requires further dose reduction, the patient must be discontinued from abemaciclib.

data.name: Discontinuation for IDFS Event
data.text: The patient experiences any of the IDFS events as per STEEP criteria. Study treatment must be discontinued.

data.name: Discontinuation for Significant Noncompliance
data.text: Patients who are significantly noncompliant with abemaciclib may be discontinued from abemaciclib treatment. A patient will be considered significantly noncompliant if she or he repeatedly misses more than 25% of abemaciclib doses between scheduled clinic visits.

data.name: Discontinuation for Pregnancy
data.text: The patient becomes pregnant during the study.

data.name: Discontinuation for Unacceptable Toxicity
data.text: Unacceptable toxicity as determined by investigator.

data.name: Discontinuation for Enrollment in Another Trial
data.text: The patient is enrolled in any other clinical trial involving an investigational product or any other type of medical research judged not to be scientifically or medically compatible with this study.

data.name: Abemaciclib Dose Reduction Level 1
data.text: Abemaciclib reduced from 150 mg twice daily to 100 mg twice daily for qualifying toxicity events as per dose adjustment table.

data.name: Hematologic Toxicity Grade 3 Dose Suspension
data.text: Hematologic Toxicity Grade 3: Dose MUST be suspended until toxicity resolves to at least Grade 2. Dose reduction is NOT required.

data.name: Hematologic Toxicity Recurrent Grade 3 or Grade 4 Dose Modification
data.text: Hematologic Toxicity Recurrent Grade 3 or Grade 4: Dose MUST be suspended until toxicity resolves to at least Grade 2. Dose MUST be reduced by 1 dose level.

data.name: Growth Factor Use Dose Modification
data.text: If patient requires administration of blood cell growth factors: Dose MUST be suspended for at least 48 hours after the last dose of growth factors and until toxicity resolves to at least Grade 2. Dose MUST be reduced by 1 dose level unless already performed.

data.name: Diarrhea Grade 2 Dose Suspension
data.text: Diarrhea Grade 2 that does not resolve within 24 hours to at least Grade 1: Dose MUST be suspended until toxicity resolves to at least Grade 1. Dose reduction is NOT required.

data.name: Diarrhea Persistent/Recurrent Grade 2 or Grade 3_4 Dose Modification
data.text: Diarrhea persistent or recurrent Grade 2 that does not resolve with maximal supportive measures, or requires hospitalization, or Grade 3 or 4: Dose MUST be suspended until toxicity resolves to at least Grade 1. Dose MUST be reduced by 1 dose level.

data.name: ALT/AST Grade 2_3 Dose Modification
data.text: ALT/AST Increased persistent or recurrent Grade 2 (>3.0_5.0×ULN), or Grade 3 (>5.0_20.0×ULN): Dose MUST be suspended until toxicity resolves to baseline or Grade 1. Dose MUST be reduced by 1 dose level.

data.name: ILD/Pneumonitis Grade 2 Dose Modification
data.text: ILD/pneumonitis Grade 2: Dose MUST be suspended until toxicity resolves to baseline or Grade 1. Dose MUST be reduced by 1 dose level.

data.name: Arm A Single Drug Discontinuation Continuation Rule
data.text: For Arm A only, if a patient discontinues only one of the combination drugs (abemaciclib or endocrine therapy) prior to completion of the 2_year on_study treatment period for a reason other than an IDFS event per STEEP criteria, she/he should continue the other drug until completion of the 2_year on_study treatment period or other discontinuation criteria are met, whichever occurs first.

data.name: Endocrine Therapy Switch
data.text: A switch to another standard endocrine therapy is allowed as per the investigator's discretion and in the absence of an IDFS event per STEEP criteria, during the on_study treatment period.

data.name: Early Termination _ Investigator Decision
data.description: The investigator decides that the patient should be discontinued from the study or study treatment.

data.name: Early Termination _ Patient Decision
data.description: The patient requests to be withdrawn from the study or study treatment.

data.name: Early Termination _ Pregnancy
data.description: The patient becomes pregnant during the study.

data.name: Early Termination _ Significant Noncompliance
data.description: The patient is significantly noncompliant with study procedures and/or treatment.

data.name: Early Termination _ IDFS Event
data.description: The patient experiences any of the IDFS events as per STEEP criteria.

data.name: Early Termination _ Unacceptable Toxicity
data.description: The patient experiences unacceptable toxicity requiring discontinuation of all study treatment.

data.name: Early Termination _ ALT/AST Grade 4
data.description: ALT/AST >20.0×ULN (Grade 4) requires mandatory discontinuation of abemaciclib.

data.name: Early Termination _ Hy's Law
data.description: Elevation in AST and/or ALT >3x ULN with total bilirubin >2x ULN in the absence of cholestasis requires mandatory discontinuation of abemaciclib.

data.name: Early Termination _ ILD/Pneumonitis Grade 3_4
data.description: ILD/pneumonitis Grade 3 or Grade 4 requires mandatory discontinuation of abemaciclib.

data.name: Early Termination _ Enrollment in Incompatible Trial
data.description: The patient is enrolled in any other clinical trial involving an investigational product or medical research judged not to be scientifically or medically compatible with this study.

data.name: Study Completion
data.description: Completion of the 2_year on_study treatment period followed by short_term follow_up visit and long_term follow_up through Year 10.

data.name: Early Termination _ Abemaciclib Lowest Dose Further Reduction Required
data.description: If a patient receiving the 50_mg twice daily dose of abemaciclib requires further dose reduction, the patient must be discontinued from abemaciclib.

data.name: Visit 27 End of Treatment Decision
data.description: Decision point at 24 months (Visit 27) anchored to Visit 1 to determine completion of 2_year on_study treatment period and transition to short_term follow_up.

data.name: Dose Modification Decision at Each Clinic Visit
data.description: At each clinic visit, assess toxicity and determine if dose suspension, dose reduction, or discontinuation of abemaciclib is required per dose adjustment table.

data.name: Unscheduled Visit Decision Between V26 and V27
data.description: If there are >40 days between Visit 26 and Visit 27, unscheduled visits must be scheduled to complete the 2_year period.

data.name: Single Drug Discontinuation Decision (Arm A)
data.description: For Arm A, if a patient discontinues only one drug (abemaciclib or endocrine therapy) for a reason other than an IDFS event, the other drug should continue until completion of 2_year treatment period or other discontinuation criteria are met.

data.name: Dose Suspension >28 Days Decision
data.description: For patients not recovering from toxicity within 28 days, a delay >28 days is permitted upon agreement between the investigator and the Lilly CRP/CRS and abemaciclib dose adjustment is to be considered.

data.name: Rescreening Eligibility Decision
data.description: A patient who fails screening is allowed to screen again after signing a new ICF under specified conditions: protocol amendment eligibility change, patient status change, or extenuating circumstances preventing enrollment.
